name: Atlantis Full
description: Atlantis is a legendary island civilization, first mentioned by Plato in his dialogues *Timaeus* and *Critias* (c. 360 BC). Plato describes Atlantis as a powerful, technologically advanced, and morally corrupt naval empire that attempted to conquer Athens, but was ultimately defeated and subsequently sank into the Atlantic Ocean in a single day and night of misfortune. While Plato presented the story as historical, many scholars believe it was an allegory to illustrate his philosophical ideas about ideal societies and the dangers of hubris and materialism. Proponents of a historical Atlantis interpret Plato's account literally, searching for its geographical location and evidence of its existence. They often point to alleged similarities between ancient cultures on both sides of the Atlantic, such as pyramid building, shared myths, and advanced technologies, as evidence of Atlantean influence. Some propose that Atlantis was a global civilization with colonies worldwide. Mainstream archaeology and history generally dismiss Atlantis as a myth or philosophical construct. The lack of geological or archaeological evidence supporting its existence is a primary reason for skepticism. Scientists argue that there is no plausible mechanism for a large island to sink suddenly and completely as described by Plato. Furthermore, the advanced technology attributed to Atlantis is inconsistent with the known archaeological record of the Bronze Age. Despite the lack of scientific support, the legend of Atlantis continues to capture the imagination and inspire various interpretations, from New Age philosophies to conspiracy theories.
subtitle: The legendary lost island civilization.

claims.claim: Atlantis was a real and advanced civilization.
claims.evidence: Similarities between ancient cultures, unexplained artifacts, and alleged psychic readings.
claims.criticism: Lack of archaeological evidence, misinterpretations of historical data, and the unreliability of psychic claims.

claims.claim: Atlantis possessed advanced technology, including anti-gravity and crystal power.
claims.evidence: Speculative interpretations of ancient texts and architectural structures.
claims.criticism: No physical evidence of such technology has ever been found, and the interpretations are often based on pseudoscientific ideas.

claims.claim: The sinking of Atlantis caused a global catastrophe and influenced the development of later civilizations.
claims.evidence: Flood myths in various cultures and alleged sudden shifts in climate.
claims.criticism: Flood myths are common in many cultures and likely arose independently, and climate shifts have natural explanations.

keyFigures.name: Plato
keyFigures.role: First documented source of the Atlantis story.

keyFigures.name: Ignatius L. Donnelly
keyFigures.role: Popularized the idea of a historical Atlantis in his book *Atlantis: The Antediluvian World*.

keyFigures.name: Kenneth Feder
keyFigures.role: Archaeologist and critic of Atlantis theories, author of *Frauds, Myths, and Mysteries: Science and Pseudoscience in Archaeology*.

timeline.event: Ignatius L. Donnelly publishes *Atlantis: The Antediluvian World*, popularizing the historical Atlantis theory.
Atlantis becomes a popular topic in esoteric and New Age literature.

sources.author: Kenneth Feder
sources.work: Frauds, Myths, and Mysteries: Science and Pseudoscience in Archaeology
sources.title: Kenneth Feder

sources.author: Charles Pellegrino
sources.work: Ghosts of Vesuvius: A New Look at the Last Days of Pompeii, How Towers Fall, and Other Geological Catastrophes
sources.title: Charles Pellegrino
